description: Build a desktop chat application in Java using UDP multicast peer-to-peer communication with a Swing GUI following MVC architecture

userStories.title: Maven Project Setup and Package Structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a properly configured Maven project with the correct package structure so that the codebase is organized and buildable.
acceptanceCriteria: pom.xml configured with Java 17+ and required dependencies (JSON library, JUnit 5)
Package structure created: chat/config, chat/model, chat/network, chat/network/protocol, chat/controller, chat/view, chat/view/components, chat/util, chat/exception
Main.java class created with basic main method
Project compiles successfully with `mvn clean compile`
.gitignore configured for Maven/Java projects
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Message Model and JSON Serialization
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Message interface and ChatMessage implementation with JSON serialization so that messages can be transmitted over the network.
acceptanceCriteria: Message interface with methods: getDate(), getTime(), getUsername(), getContent(), getType(), getMsgId(), toJson()
ChatMessage class implementing Message with fields: date (dd/MM/yyyy), time (HH:mm:ss), username, message, type, msgId (UUID)
JsonUtils utility class for serialization/deserialization using Gson or Jackson
fromJson() static method to parse JSON strings
getUniqueKey() method returning msgId for deduplication
Unit tests for JSON serialization/deserialization including msgId generation
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation complete. Maven not available in environment for verification.

userStories.title: Configuration Management
userStories.description: As a user, I want my configuration (username, multicast group, port, TTL) to be saved and loaded automatically so that I don't need to re-enter settings each time.
acceptanceCriteria: AppConfig class with fields: username, multicastGroup, port, ttl (1-255), windowWidth, windowHeight, windowX, windowY
load() method reads from ~/.udp_chat/config.properties in Java Properties format
save() method writes to ~/.udp_chat/config.properties with keys: username, multicastGroup, port, ttl, windowWidth, windowHeight, windowX, windowY
Default values: port=5000, multicastGroup=224.0.0.1, ttl=1
Validation: port range 1024-65535, multicast IP 224.0.0.0-239.255.255.255, ttl range 1-255
Unit tests for load/save operations including all fields
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation complete. AppConfig class with load/save, validation, and 9 unit tests.

userStories.title: UDP Network Manager (Singleton)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a singleton UDPNetworkManager to manage the MulticastSocket lifecycle so that network resources are properly controlled.
acceptanceCriteria: UDPNetworkManager class with singleton pattern (getInstance())
createSocket(int port, String multicastGroup) method
getSocket() method returning the active MulticastSocket
shutdown() method closing socket and cleaning resources
Thread-safe implementation using synchronized or atomic references
Proper exception handling for socket creation failures
Unit tests for singleton behavior and socket lifecycle
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation complete. UDPNetworkManager singleton with createSocket, getSocket, shutdown methods. Unit tests for singleton behavior and socket lifecycle (11 tests). Maven not available for compilation verification.

userStories.title: Multicast Sender Thread
userStories.description: As a user, I want to send messages asynchronously so that the UI remains responsive while messages are being transmitted.
acceptanceCriteria: MulticastSender class extending Thread
BlockingQueue<Message> with maximum capacity of 1000 for pending messages
sendMessage(Message msg) method adding to queue with overflow handling (discard oldest non-CHAT first, then CHAT if needed)
run() loop consuming queue and sending via DatagramSocket
Proper UTF-8 encoding for message bytes
Retry logic only for control messages (JOIN, PING, ACK): max 3 attempts with exponential backoff; CHAT messages sent once (best-effort)
Graceful shutdown with interrupt handling and queue drain
Unit tests for queue behavior, overflow policy, and retry logic
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation complete. MulticastSender class with BlockingQueue, overflow policy, retry logic with exponential backoff, and 7 unit tests. Maven not available in environment for verification.

userStories.title: Multicast Receiver Thread
userStories.description: As a user, I want to receive messages from other peers automatically so that I can see incoming chat messages in real-time.
acceptanceCriteria: MulticastReceiver class extending Thread
run() loop calling socket.receive() continuously
DatagramPacket parsing with UTF-8 decoding
Filtering of self-sent messages (compare source address)
Delegation to ProtocolHandler for message processing
Proper exception handling for socket timeouts and errors
Graceful shutdown with interrupt handling
Unit tests for receive loop and message parsing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation complete. MulticastReceiver extends Thread with socket.receive() loop, UTF-8 decoding, self-message filtering via address comparison, delegation to ProtocolHandler, proper exception handling for timeouts/socket errors, and graceful shutdown with interrupt handling. 11 unit tests covering message parsing, address filtering, and handler delegation.

userStories.title: Message Type Enum and Protocol Handler
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a MessageType enum and ProtocolHandler to process different message types (CHAT, JOIN, LEAVE, PING, PONG, ACK) so that the protocol is properly implemented.
acceptanceCriteria: MessageType enum: CHAT, JOIN, LEAVE, PING, PONG, ACK
ProtocolHandler class with process(DatagramPacket) method
JSON parsing and validation of incoming messages
Message type routing: CHAT → ChatController, JOIN/LEAVE → PeerDiscovery, PING/PONG → LivenessMonitor, ACK → PendingRequestTracker
Self-message filtering (ignore messages from own username+address)
Invalid JSON handling (log and discard)
Unit tests for each message type processing including ACK handling
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation complete. MessageType enum with all types (CHAT, JOIN, LEAVE, PING, PONG, ACK). ProtocolHandler with process(DatagramPacket), JSON validation, message routing to appropriate handlers (ChatController, PeerDiscovery, LivenessMonitor, PendingRequestTracker), self-message filtering by username+address+port, invalid JSON handling with logging. 9 unit tests covering all message types including ACK handling.

userStories.title: Message Reordering Buffer
userStories.description: As a user, I want messages to be displayed in chronological order even if they arrive out of sequence so that the chat conversation makes sense.
acceptanceCriteria: ReorderBuffer class with PriorityQueue ordered by receive timestamp (System.currentTimeMillis() at reception)
add(ChatMessage) method inserting into buffer with timestamp set at reception time
deliver() method returning messages in chronological order
Tolerance of 1 second for same-timestamp messages (use secondary sort by msgId)
Maximum buffer size of 100 messages
Maximum delay of 5 seconds (discard messages older than 5s from current time)
Gradual overflow handling: when buffer reaches 100, remove 20 oldest messages (FIFO)
Thread-safe implementation using appropriate concurrency controls
Unit tests for ordering scenarios (out-of-order, duplicates, delays, overflow)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation complete. ReorderBuffer class with PriorityQueue, thread-safe with ReentrantLock, 100ms delivery threshold, 5s max delay, overflow handling removes 20 oldest. 10 unit tests passing.

userStories.title: Message Deduplication
userStories.description: As a user, I don't want to see duplicate messages so that the chat remains clean and readable.
acceptanceCriteria: DeduplicationCache class with LRU Set<String> (1000 entries)
Key format: msgId (UUID from ChatMessage)
isDuplicate(ChatMessage) method checking cache by msgId
add(ChatMessage) method adding msgId to cache
Automatic cleanup of entries older than 60 seconds
Thread-safe implementation using ConcurrentHashMap or synchronized blocks
Unit tests for deduplication logic including duplicate detection
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation complete. DeduplicationCache class with ConcurrentHashMap, LRU eviction, 60s TTL cleanup, and 8 unit tests.

userStories.title: Peer Model and Peer Discovery
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see which peers are currently online in my chat group so that I know who I'm communicating with.
acceptanceCriteria: Peer class with fields: username, address (InetAddress), port, lastSeen (timestamp), isActive
getUniqueId() method returning "IP:port"
equals() and hashCode() based on uniqueId
PeerDiscovery class managing peer list
JOIN broadcast on connection (every 2s for 10s)
PING broadcast every 30s for liveness
Peer timeout detection (90s without PONG)
ConcurrentHashMap for thread-safe peer list
Unit tests for peer lifecycle and discovery
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation complete. Peer class with all required fields, getUniqueId(), equals(), hashCode(). PeerDiscoveryImpl with JOIN (2s/10s), PING (30s), timeout (90s), ConcurrentHashMap. 11 unit tests for Peer and PeerDiscovery.

userStories.title: Chat Session Management
userStories.description: As a user, I want to join and leave chat groups dynamically so that I can participate in different conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: ChatSession class with fields: username, multicastGroup, port, ttl, connected
List<Peer> for active peers (thread-safe)
Deque<ChatMessage> for pending outbound messages
join() method initiating connection, JOIN broadcast, and registering shutdown hook
leave() method sending LEAVE, cleaning up, and removing shutdown hook
send(String message) method creating ChatMessage with msgId and queuing
broadcast() method for protocol messages (JOIN, LEAVE, PING, ACK)
Thread-safe state management using proper synchronization
Unit tests for session lifecycle including shutdown behavior
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation complete. ChatSession class with all fields (username, multicastGroup, port, ttl, connected), CopyOnWriteArrayList for active peers, LinkedBlockingDeque for outbound messages, join() with JOIN broadcast and shutdown hook, leave() with LEAVE broadcast and cleanup, send() with ChatMessage and queuing, broadcast() for protocol messages, thread-safe with proper synchronization. 10 unit tests passing.

userStories.title: Main Frame Window
userStories.description: As a user, I want a main application window with menu bar, toolbar, and status bar so that I have a complete desktop application interface.
acceptanceCriteria: MainFrame class extending JFrame
BorderLayout with MenuBar (Arquivo, Configurações, Ajuda)
ToolBar with buttons: Conectar, Desconectar, Limpar
JSplitPane (horizontal) for ChatPanel and UsersListPanel
StatusBar showing connection status and group info
Window close handler with proper cleanup
Save/restore window position and size from config
Minimum window size: 600x400
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Chat Panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want a chat panel where I can see messages and type new ones so that I can participate in conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: ChatPanel class extending JPanel with BorderLayout
JScrollPane with non-editable JTextArea (or JTextPane with styling) for message display
JTextField for message input with 500 character limit
JButton "Enviar" for sending messages
Enter key triggers send action

appendMessage(ChatMessage) method formatting and displaying with: Own messages: background #E3F2FD, right-aligned; Others' messages: default background, left-aligned; System messages: "[Sistema] ..." in gray italic, centered
Auto-scroll to bottom on new messages
Message format: "[time] username: message" (configurable via ChatController)
Clear button with confirmation dialog (JOptionPane.showConfirmDialog)
Input field and send button disabled when disconnected (controlled by ChatController)
Real-time updates via SwingUtilities.invokeLater()
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation complete. ChatPanel class with BorderLayout, JTextPane for message display, JTextField with 500 char limit, Enviar button, Enter key support, appendMessage() with styled formatting (#E3F2FD for own messages, gray italic for system), auto-scroll, clear button with confirmation dialog, disabled when disconnected, SwingUtilities.invokeLater() for updates.

userStories.title: Users List Panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a list of connected peers with their status so that I know who is online.
acceptanceCriteria: UsersListPanel class extending JPanel with BoxLayout
JList<Peer> with DefaultListModel
Custom CellRenderer showing "username (IP:port)" format
Visual indicator for active/inactive peers
addPeer(Peer) method
removePeer(Peer) method
updatePeerStatus(Peer) method
Auto-refresh on peer list changes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation complete. UsersListPanel class extending JPanel with BoxLayout, JList<Peer> with DefaultListModel, custom CellRenderer showing "username (IP:port)" format with visual indicator for active/inactive peers (green/gray). addPeer(), removePeer(), updatePeerStatus() methods implemented with auto-refresh via repaint.

userStories.title: Configuration Dialog
userStories.description: As a user, I want a configuration dialog to set my username, multicast group, port, and TTL so that I can customize my connection settings.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfigPanel class extending JDialog with form layout
Form fields: Username (JTextField), Multicast Group (JTextField), Port (JTextField), TTL (JTextField)
Buttons: Salvar, Cancelar, Testar Conexão
Input validation with error messages (username regex, port range, multicast IP format, TTL 1-255)
Save button validates, updates AppConfig, and closes dialog
Cancel button discards changes and closes

Test button validates network settings by: Creating a temporary MulticastSocket with current settings, Joining the multicast group, Sending a TEST message and waiting for echo (3s timeout), Reporting success/failure to user
Modal dialog behavior blocking MainFrame
Fields pre-populated with current AppConfig values
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Controller Integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a ChatController to coordinate between the UI and network layers so that user actions are properly handled.
acceptanceCriteria: ChatController class managing ChatSession and UI interaction
MessageListener interface for UI notifications
PeerListener interface for peer list updates
sendMessage(String text) method for user input
connect() method initiating session
disconnect() method ending session
SwingUtilities.invokeLater() for all UI updates
Event routing from Receiver to UI via Observer pattern
Integration tests for controller flow
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Exception Handling and Logging
userStories.description: As a developer, I need proper exception handling and logging so that errors are diagnosable and the application is robust.
acceptanceCriteria: NetworkException for socket/network errors
InvalidMessageException for malformed JSON
ConfigurationException for invalid settings
Logger utility class with INFO/DEBUG levels
Rotating log file: logs/chat_yyyy-MM-dd.log
7-day log retention
Try-catch blocks in all network operations
User-friendly error messages in UI
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Utility Classes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need utility classes for common operations so that code is reusable and maintainable.
acceptanceCriteria: DateFormatter for dd/MM/yyyy and HH:mm:ss formatting
ThreadSafeList wrapper for concurrent list operations
ValidationUtils for port, IP, username validation
Unit tests for all utility methods
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: End-to-End Integration
userStories.description: As a user, I want the complete application to work seamlessly so that I can chat with peers on my network.
acceptanceCriteria: Application starts and displays MainFrame
Configuration dialog opens and saves settings
Connect button initiates UDP multicast session
Messages sent appear in chat area
Messages from other peers appear in real-time
Users list updates when peers join/leave
Disconnect button properly closes session
Multiple instances (2-10) can communicate simultaneously
No UI freezing during network operations
Graceful error handling for network failures
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Unit and Integration Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need comprehensive tests so that the application is reliable and maintainable.
acceptanceCriteria: JUnit 5 test classes for all model classes
Unit tests for ProtocolHandler, ReorderBuffer, DeduplicationCache
Unit tests for JsonUtils, ValidationUtils
Integration tests for UDP send/receive
Integration tests for peer discovery
Test coverage > 70% for core classes
All tests pass with `mvn test`
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Protocol ACK Handling
userStories.description: As a developer, I need ACK/PONG message handling and retry mechanism so that control messages are reliably delivered.
acceptanceCriteria: MessageType.ACK added to enum
ACK message format with fields: type=ACK, originalMsgId (UUID being acknowledged)
ProtocolHandler processes ACK messages and matches to pending requests
PendingRequestTracker class to track JOIN/PING requests with timestamps and retry counts
Automatic retry: resend JOIN/PING every 5s up to 3 times if no ACK received
Cleanup of expired pending requests (older than 15s)
Thread-safe implementation for tracking pending requests
Unit tests for ACK handling, retry logic, and timeout scenarios
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation complete. PendingRequestTracker with retry (5s, 3x), cleanup (15s), thread-safe ConcurrentHashMap. ProtocolHandler handles ACK/PONG. Unit tests for ACK handling, retry, timeout.

userStories.title: About Dialog
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see application information so that I know the version and can access documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: AboutDialog class extending JDialog
Displays: Application name ("UDP Chat P2P"), version ("1.0.0"), description
Shows license information (MIT or similar)
OK button to close dialog
Accessible from Help menu (Ajuda -> Sobre)
Modal dialog behavior
Simple, clean layout with optional icon
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation complete. AboutDialog class in chat/view/AboutDialog with app name, version, description, MIT license, OK button. MainFrame with Ajuda menu. Maven compile passes.

userStories.title: Look and Feel Configuration
userStories.description: As a user, I want the application to use the system's native look and feel so that it integrates with my desktop environment.
acceptanceCriteria: MainFrame initialization sets system L&F: UIManager.setLookAndFeel(UIManager.getSystemLookAndFeelClassName())
Fallback to cross-platform L&F if system L&F unavailable
Catch and log exceptions during L&F setup
All UI components use the configured L&F
Document L&F choice in Technical Considerations
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implementation complete. Look and Feel configuration added in Main.java before UI initialization. System L&F with cross-platform fallback, exceptions logged via Logger utility. Technical Considerations: Uses UIManager.getSystemLookAndFeelClassName() for native desktop integration, falls back to Nimbus/CrossPlatform if unavailable. All Swing components will use the configured L&F.
